old_claim.note: Insurance Claims Adjustment provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because the scoped registry documents vessel identity, ownership, dimensions, port, and registration changes, while the insurance node is specifically fire-insurance claims assessment.

source_shape.source_locator: The registration guide says shipping registers contain ship dimensions, tonnage, rig, owners, builders, masters, ownership changes, and fate, and do not show financial information except mortgages.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source describes ship-registration contents without making insurance claims assessment part of the registry process.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The existing insurance_claims_adjustment node is scoped to fire-insurance claim settlement, so it is not evidenced as a direct prerequisite for Custom House ship registration.

ontology_before: Merchant Marine Registry depended directly on Early Insurance Claims Assessment, implying fire-insurance claims procedures enabled ship registration.
ontology_after: Merchant Ship Registration is scoped to Custom House vessel registration and no longer depends on insurance-claims assessment.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: merchant_marine_registry no longer directly depends on insurance_claims_adjustment.
would_reject_if: The node were rescoped to marine insurance underwriting or insurance classification registers.
A stronger source showed fire-insurance claims assessment directly enabled Custom House merchant ship registration.
The insurance_claims_adjustment node were broadened away from fire-insurance loss adjustment into all maritime ownership documentation.
short_reason: Fire-insurance claims assessment is not a direct prerequisite for ship registration.
